_meta.description: Audit finding template for NACSA Act 854 CoP compliance audits using the 5 C's framework

findingTemplate.title: [Concise finding title - max 100 chars]

condition.guidance: Describe the current state objectively. Include specific quantities, dates, and system names. Reference evidence examined.

criteria.guidance: Cite the specific Act section, CoP requirement, directive provision, or internal policy that establishes the obligation.

cause.guidance: Explain why the gap exists. Common root causes: lack of awareness, resource constraints, process gaps, technology limitations, organizational change.

consequence.guidance: Describe the potential impact in terms relevant to NCII: national security, essential service disruption, data compromise, regulatory penalties.

correctiveAction.guidance: Provide actionable, specific recommendations. Include both immediate actions and longer-term improvements.

findingTemplate.auditorNotes: [Internal auditor working notes - not included in final report]

Critical.definition: Finding poses an immediate and significant risk to the confidentiality, integrity, or availability of NCII systems. May indicate active compromise or imminent threat.
examples: No incident response plan exists for NCII systems
Privileged access to NCII systems uses shared accounts with no MFA
Critical NCII systems have unpatched vulnerabilities with known active exploits
No backups exist for NCII systems or backups are untested for >12 months
NCII entity has not registered with NACSA despite designation
Critical.nacsaAction: Immediate notification to CE; follow-up audit may be directed

High.definition: Significant gap in control design or operation that materially weakens the entity's NCII cyber security posture.
examples: Access reviews not conducted for >6 months on NCII systems
Incident notification procedures exist but have never been tested
Penetration testing not conducted in the past 12 months
Risk assessment methodology does not cover all NCII systems
Board has not been briefed on NCII obligations
High.nacsaAction: Included in audit report; follow-up audit may be required

Medium.definition: Notable gap that should be addressed but does not pose an immediate significant risk to NCII operations.
examples: Security awareness training completion rate below 80%
Asset inventory exists but not reconciled in >6 months
Change management process lacks formal security impact assessment step
Certificate management is manual with some expired certificates found
Vendor risk assessments conducted but not on a regular schedule
Medium.nacsaAction: Included in audit report; tracked in next audit cycle

Low.definition: Minor improvement opportunity that would strengthen the entity's compliance posture.
examples: Policy documents lack version control metadata
Training records maintained in spreadsheets rather than centralized system
Network diagrams current but missing some recently added segments
Backup monitoring alerts configured but notification list needs updating
Configuration baselines documented but not yet automated
Low.nacsaAction: Noted in audit report as observation
